steps.title: Find the shared pattern
steps.content: The passage defines a loanword and then explains that English received coyote from Nahuatl through Spanish and condor from Quechua through Spanish. The main idea is the shared Indigenous origin of both English words.

steps.title: Reject overbroad statements
steps.content: Choice B makes an unsupported comparison about Spanish borrowing from Nahuatl and Quechua. Choice C reverses the borrowing direction. Choice D introduces meaning change, which the passage does not discuss. The answer is A.
